ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flagged. Card already had characters: [theros-pantheon] in frontmatter; trivia confirms and elaborates on Mogis pantheon connection.

trivia_bullets.text: Mogis is the God of Slaughter on Theros — dark twin of Iroas, patron deity of all Theros minotaurs. He appears as a massive four-horned minotaur in spiked bronze armor wielding an ebon greataxe. Mogis's worship involves brutal rites and ritual self-mutilation, particularly during the blood moon (his 'crimson eye'). This card is a direct expression of that cult — an Enchantment Creature (Minotaur Shaman) whose ability is sacrifice-to-draw. Wires to [[theros-pantheon]].
sources: WebSearch snippet: MTG Wiki: Mogis via Fandom

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'We offer to Mogis the blood of the weak, and in return he makes us strong.' The transactional theology — sacrificial tithe upward, martial power downward — is Mogis's defining relationship with followers, and the card's oracle text enacts it mechanically: sacrifice a creature, draw a card.

As an Enchantment Creature, Soulreaper of Mogis triggers Constellation on entry. Its sacrifice effect ({2}{B}, sac a creature, draw a card) is a card-advantage engine in black sacrifice strategies. Hipsters of the Coast rated it C, describing it as a time-buying option during board stalls.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Every Common in Theros Beyond Death Reviewed

related_cards.name: Slaughter Priest of Mogis
related_cards.note: The set's second Mogis-cult minotaur; red-black variant with haste and sacrifice synergy

related_cards.name: Mogis, God of Slaughter
related_cards.note: The god himself, legendary Enchantment Creature; direct theological lord of this card

related_cards.name: Iroas, God of Victory
related_cards.note: Mogis's divine twin; the pair define the dual nature of war in Theros canon
